title: Portal Space taps Quindar for ground mission support of its maneuvering spacecraft
summary: Quindar, a Denver-based startup, will provide mission management services for Portal Space Systems' maneuverable spacecraft missions. The agreement focuses on ground mission support for upcoming space operations.

title: Vast announces line of high-power satellite buses
summary: Vast, a commercial space station developer, is expanding into satellite manufacturing by introducing a line of high-power satellite buses. This move marks a diversification of the company's activities within the space industry.

title: Lynk and Anterix get FCC nod to test satellite D2D for private utility networks
summary: Lynk Global has received FCC approval to test direct-to-device satellite communications for private utility networks using Anterix's terrestrial spectrum in the U.S. The trial aims to enhance connectivity for utility infrastructure.

title: Anderson confirmed as NASA deputy administrator
summary: The U.S. Senate confirmed Matt Anderson as NASA's deputy administrator on May 18, making him the second-in-command of the space agency.

title: ESA-China SMILE mission lifts off to deliver first global images of Earth’s magnetosphere
summary: The SMILE mission, a joint project by the European Space Agency and China, has successfully launched to provide the first global images of Earth's magnetosphere. The mission follows over a decade of preparation and collaboration.

title: European imaging companies step in to fill warzone gap
summary: European Earth-observation firms are stepping in to provide satellite imagery of conflict zones like Iran and the Gulf region after U.S. companies reduced their coverage. This shift highlights a growing role for European players in geospatial intelligence amid regional tensions.

title: Four NASA payloads to fly on Astrolab’s first lunar rover
summary: Astrolab's first lunar rover will carry four NASA payloads on a mission planned to launch later this year. This marks a significant collaboration between Astrolab and NASA for lunar exploration.

title: Inside Golden Dome’s push to court commercial tech firms and investors
summary: Pentagon officials are promoting Golden Dome as a modern technology platform rather than a traditional weapons system to attract commercial space firms, investors, and software companies. The initiative aims to modernize missile defense by leveraging commercial tech expertise.

title: Tomorrow.io adds $35 million to DeepSky funding round
summary: Weather intelligence provider Tomorrow.io raised an additional $35 million for its DeepSky funding round, bringing the total to $210 million to advance its next-generation atmospheric data constellation.

title: House bill restores funding for TraCSS
summary: A House appropriations bill aims to restore funding for the civil space traffic management system (TraCSS), reversing an administration decision to halt its development.

title: Galactic starlight will take your breath away | Space photo of the day for May 19, 2026
summary: The James Webb Space Telescope captured a stunning image of galaxy M77, showcasing its breathtaking beauty. This space photo was featured as the 'Space photo of the day' on May 19, 2026.

title: As 'The Mandalorian and Grogu" prepare for their close-up, can 'Star Wars' ever feel like a big-screen event again?
summary: The article discusses the upcoming release of 'The Mandalorian and Grogu' and questions whether 'Star Wars' can regain its former prominence as a major theatrical experience. It reflects on how Disney+ spin-offs may have diminished the franchise's appeal in theaters.

title: Scientists just solved a tricky asteroid-hopping spacecraft riddle
summary: Scientists have developed a new mathematical approach to solve the complex trajectory problem for asteroid-hopping spacecraft, addressing a challenge akin to the Traveling Salesperson problem. This breakthrough enables optimal planning for visiting multiple asteroids.

title: The UFO files: What did we learn from the Pentagon's 1st big release?
summary: The Pentagon released its first batch of UFO files on May 8, prompting analysis from experts to interpret the data. The release has generated significant public and scientific interest in understanding the contents and implications of these documents.

title: Scientists found stardust trapped in Antarctic ice. What could it tell us about our solar system?
summary: Scientists discovered stardust trapped in Antarctic ice, which may reveal insights about the solar system. The dust can penetrate the solar system's shielding and reach Earth.

title: Why bringing back 'The Mandalorian and Grogu' isn't the way for 'Star Wars'
summary: The article argues that 'The Mandalorian and Grogu' should not have been released theatrically and questions its existence. The piece criticizes the decision to bring back the characters in a theatrical format.

title: What time is SpaceX's Starship V3 launch on May 21? (Starship Flight 12 timeline)
summary: SpaceX is scheduled to launch its first Starship V3 vehicle on Flight 12 on May 21. This marks a significant milestone in the development of the Starship program.

title: Vega C rocket launches European-Chinese space weather satellite to orbit
summary: A Vega C rocket successfully launched the European-Chinese SMILE space weather satellite into orbit on May 18. This mission aims to study the interaction between Earth's magnetosphere and solar wind.

title: This amazing NASA video shows the exact moment the Artemis 2 Orion capsule broke free of its service module, and we can't stop watching it
summary: A NASA video captures the moment the Artemis 2 Orion capsule separated from its service module before re-entering Earth's atmosphere on April 10, 2026. The footage highlights a critical phase of the spacecraft's mission.

title: NASA’s New Shock Detectives Project Invites Volunteers to Help Study Solar Wind
summary: NASA's new 'Shock Detectives' project invites volunteers to analyze solar wind data from the Magnetospheric Multiscale (MMS) mission, which has collected over a decade of information. This crowdsourced effort aims to help scientists identify chaotic and peaceful regions in the data.

title: Curiosity Blog, Sols 4893-4899: Drilling at Campo Marte and a Visit From the Psyche Spacecraft
summary: NASA's Curiosity rover is preparing to drill at a new target named 'Campo Marte' on Mars, following the successful freeing of its arm from a previous block. The drill target represents a similar geologic stratum to the 'Atacama' block, located in a layered sulfate unit.

title: Johnson Photographers Honored for Award-Winning Portraits
summary: Three photographers at NASA's Johnson Space Center won top honors in the portrait category at the 2025 NASA Imagery Experts Program Annual Awards for their award-winning visual storytelling. Their work highlights collaboration and precision, as praised by Johnson Space Center Director Vanessa Wyche.

title: NASA’s MAVEN Makes 1st Discovery of Atmospheric Effect at Mars
summary: NASA's MAVEN mission discovered an atmospheric effect on Mars that was previously only observed in Earth's magnetosphere. The phenomenon involves charged particles being compressed in a manner similar to squeezing toothpaste.

title: NASA Selects Next Class of Space Health Postdoctoral Fellows
summary: NASA's Translational Research Institute for Space Health selected two new postdoctoral fellows to research space food systems and astronaut eye health. The fellows will begin their projects in May under the TRISH Postdoctoral Fellowship Program.

title: Beacon of Light
summary: NASA's James Webb Space Telescope captured an image of galaxy M77's heart, revealing bright light caused by gas orbiting a central black hole. The gas heats up due to rapid motion in the black hole's strong gravitational field.

title: NASA Science, Cargo Launch on 34th SpaceX Resupply Mission to Station
summary: NASA's 34th SpaceX resupply mission launched to the International Space Station with new scientific experiments. The mission lifted off on a Falcon 9 rocket from Cape Canaveral Space Force Station.

title: Curiosity Shakes Loose a Pesky Rock
summary: NASA's Curiosity Mars rover successfully removed a stuck rock from its drill after several days of attempts. The rock was dislodged on May 1, 2026, following repositioning and vibration techniques.

title: Hubble Sights Galaxy in Transition
summary: NASA's Hubble Space Telescope captured an image of a galaxy with a bright center and hints of a spiral structure, though it lacks distinct spiral arms. The galaxy's face is partially obscured by reddish-brown dust filaments, with light from distant galaxies visible in the background.

title: Vega C set to launch SMILE to study Earth’s magnetosphere
summary: ESA's SMILE mission is set to launch aboard Vega C to study Earth's magnetosphere. The mission aims to investigate the interaction between solar wind and Earth's magnetic field.

title: Super Heavy Block 3 the Booster of the Future
summary: The article discusses the evolution of the Super Heavy Block 3 booster, the first stage of SpaceX's Starship, highlighting its ongoing modifications. It is presented as a key development in SpaceX's future launch capabilities.

title: Artemis III deep into processing and planning for key HLS test
summary: NASA is actively planning and processing Artemis III, a significant crewed mission to the Moon. The mission includes key Human Landing System (HLS) testing.

title: Flight 12 readies for the debut SpaceX’s next Ship evolution
summary: SpaceX is preparing for the debut of its next iteration of the Starship second stage, following multiple evolutions since the early test flights of SN8. Flight 12 is expected to showcase the latest advancements in the spacecraft's design.

title: Rocket Lab signs new launch contracts and acquires robotics company during Q1 2026
summary: Rocket Lab achieved record-breaking performance in Q1 2026 by signing new launch contracts and acquiring a robotics company. The company continued its growth trajectory from 2025.

title: SpaceX’s new Starship pad readies for first launch
summary: SpaceX has completed construction of a redesigned launch pad for its Starship rocket, which is now preparing for its first launch. The new pad represents a significant upgrade to support future missions.

title: SpaceX CRS-34 launches to ISS on third attempt
summary: SpaceX successfully launched the CRS-34 mission to the ISS on its third attempt. The launch occurred just over two weeks after the Progress MS-34 docked to the station.

title: Launch Preview: SpaceX and Chinese missions fill busy launch manifest
summary: SpaceX and China have a busy launch schedule with nine launches planned for the week of May 11. The launches span multiple missions across both countries.

title: Full Stack Flight 12 completes WDR
summary: Full Stack Flight 12 completed a Wet Dress Rehearsal (WDR) after Booster 19's successful static fire test. The event was reported by NASASpaceFlight.com.

title: China prepares cargo, crew, and deep space missions, as commercial sector steps towards reusability
summary: China is preparing to launch cargo and crew missions to its Tiangong space station while advancing deep space missions. The country's commercial sector is also making progress toward reusability.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: TEC Business to Business website is mentioned twice without additional context in the provided news snippet.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates its 50th anniversary by releasing a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements over the past five decades. The publication underscores the agency's contributions to space exploration and technology.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: ESA's Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section tests materials for spacecraft to ensure they survive space conditions. The section is supported by young researchers contributing innovative ideas.

title: Extended Reality at ESA opens new pathways for space exploration
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is leveraging Extended Reality (XR) for training, operations, simulations, and public engagement in space exploration. This initiative aims to enhance both technical and educational aspects of space missions.

title: Sensitive and sturdy
summary: The article discusses the development of a sensitive and sturdy component for space applications. The focus is on its durability and precision in harsh space environments.

title: The great parachute bake-out
summary: The article discusses a critical test involving a parachute system for a space mission, referred to as 'the great parachute bake-out.' This test likely involves exposing the parachute to extreme conditions to ensure its reliability.

title: Baking a parachute for Mars
summary: ESA is sterilizing a 35-meter parachute for the ExoMars Rosalind Franklin rover mission to ensure it is 10,000 times cleaner than a smartphone, preventing microbial contamination on Mars. The parachute, made of nylon and Kevlar, will slow the rover during its 2028 landing to search for signs of life.

title: A new grip on space: electrostatic capture technology
summary: A new electrostatic capture technology has been developed for space applications, enabling secure handling of objects in microgravity. This innovation could revolutionize satellite servicing and debris removal.

title: Plato aces space-like tests
summary: The European Space Agency's Plato mission has passed critical space-like tests, confirming its readiness for a 2027 launch to search for Earth-like planets.

title: SME Forum 2026 – Join ESA's annual consultation process
summary: ESA invites SMEs and midcaps to participate in its annual consultation process through the SME Forum 2026, held in Paris on June 8-9, 2026. The event includes an online preparatory forum in April and May.

title: Vast Launches Satellite Bus Business Line
summary: Vast, a commercial space station company, announced the launch of a new satellite bus business line. The initiative targets low-cost, high-power satellite missions across communications, Earth observation, and national security.

title: York Space Systems to Acquire Solestial
summary: York Space Systems is acquiring Solestial, marking a significant move in the space industry. This acquisition highlights York Space Systems' active M&A strategy in 2024.

title: Astrolab Unveils Payloads Flying on FLIP Lunar Mission
summary: Astrolab has announced the payloads that will fly on its upcoming FLIP lunar mission, scheduled for this year. The mission will carry a variety of scientific payloads onboard a lunar rover.

title: Potomac Database Systems Unveils Plans to Amass Lunar Data
summary: Potomac Database Systems has announced plans to collect and store lunar data, highlighting commercial opportunities beyond traditional lunar activities like mining. The initiative aims to leverage data as a valuable resource in space exploration.

title: U-Space Selects Skynopy to Support Multiple Missions
summary: U-Space has selected Skynopy to support multiple missions using its ground-station-as-a-service model. Skynopy's service is gaining significant traction in the space industry.

title: Intuitive Machines Bets on Lunar Comms with Goonhilly Buy
summary: Intuitive Machines acquired UK-based Goonhilly Earth Station and its US subsidiary COMSAT to bolster its lunar communications capabilities. The deal aims to provide integrated deep-space communication and PNT solutions for customers.

title: Iridium Boosts Air Safety Offering With Aireon Acquisition
summary: Iridium has acquired Aireon to enhance its air safety services by integrating space-based VHF communications. This move aims to strengthen Iridium's offerings in aviation safety and tracking.

title: European Space Startups Jockey for ‘Infrastructure’ Financing
summary: European space startups are seeking infrastructure financing as they face challenges in securing growth-stage capital. The region's space industry struggles with limited late-stage investment compared to other regions.

title: Guetlein: CBO is ‘Not Estimating What We’re Building’
summary: The head of the Golden Dome program criticized the Congressional Budget Office's $1.2 trillion cost estimate for the missile-defense program, stating it does not accurately reflect what is being built.

title: Meet Blacknight Space Labs In-Orbit Infrastructure Accelerator
summary: Blacknight Space Labs is launching an In-Orbit Infrastructure Accelerator offering up to $100,000 per company, along with mentorship and support for defense market entry and fundraising.

title: Lynk and Anterix Target D2D for Critical Infrastructure In New Testing
summary: Lynk Global and Anterix are collaborating to test direct-to-device (D2D) communications for critical infrastructure sectors. The partnership aims to enable private D2D networks for specific use cases.

title: Skynopy to Support U-Space Missions With Ground Station Services
summary: Skynopy has secured a contract with U-Space to provide ground station services for their two satellites. U-Space, a Toulouse-based small satellite manufacturer, will rely on Skynopy for mission operations.

title: Space Force Awards Northrop Grumman $398 Million For Enhanced PTS-P
summary: The U.S. Space Force awarded Northrop Grumman a $398 million contract to develop and build an Enhanced Protected Tactical Satellite Communications-Prototype (PTS-P). This contract aims to enhance secure military satellite communications.

title: iDirectGov Releases WCore Virtualization Tool, Supporting 16 Waveforms
summary: iDirectGov has released WCore, a new virtualization tool supporting 16 waveforms, enabling rapid deployment, scaling, and management of waveforms.

title: What Will the Golden Dome Cost? Gen. Guetlein Pushes Back on CBO Cost Estimate
summary: The head of the Golden Dome program disputed a Congressional Budget Office (CBO) cost estimate for a system similar to President Trump's proposal. The disagreement highlights concerns over the financial assessment of the program.

title: IFC Market in China and India is On Cusp of Growth, Valour Predicts
summary: Valour Consultancy predicts significant growth for the In-Flight Connectivity (IFC) market in China and India. The report highlights the potential scale and expansion of these markets.

title: Cowboy Space Files for 20,000-Satellite Constellation for Data Center Services
summary: Cowboy Space has filed for a 20,000-satellite constellation to provide data center services from space, following its $275 million Series B funding round. The company, previously rebranded, aims to leverage this massive satellite network for space-based data center operations.

title: Iceye Delivers SAR Capability to Polish Armed Forces
summary: Iceye has delivered control over a radar satellite constellation to the Polish Armed Forces for sovereign intelligence. The capability was delivered in less than 12 months.

title: Kymeta Terminals to be Integrated Into Red Cat Uncrewed Surface Vessels
summary: Kymeta is partnering with Red Cat Futures to integrate its satcom terminals into uncrewed surface vessels. The collaboration was announced on May 14.

title: Major US Carries Band Together in Joint Venture to Encourage D2D Competition
summary: The three largest U.S. mobile carriers—AT&T, T-Mobile, and Verizon—have formed a joint venture to collaborate on direct-to-device (D2D) services, aiming to enhance competition in this emerging market.
